channel.title: 2026-03-22 AI × Science 文献日报 RSS
channel.description: 2026-03-22 当天筛选出的 AI × 物理 / 化学 / 材料交叉文献 RSS。

item.title: A Hall 'rectenna' can detect signals over a 100 GHz frequency range
item.description:
📚 Physics News
Many current wireless communication, imaging and sensing technologies rely on components that convert oscillating electric and magnetic fields (i.e., electromagnetic waves) into electrical signals. Some of the most used components are so-called p-n diodes, semiconducting devices that combine two types of materials with distinct electrical properties.

item.title: Snapshot-QAOA: Extending QAOA to Quantum Hamiltonian Simulation
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Reuben Tate, Quinn Langfitt, Elijah Pelofske, Ammar Kirmani, Andreas Bärtschi, John Golden 等7位作者
We present Snapshot-QAOA, a variation of the Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm (QAOA) that finds approximate minimum energy eigenstates of a large set of quantum Hamiltonians. Traditionally, QAOA targets the task of approximately solving combinatorial optimization problems. Snapshot-QAOA enables a significant expansion of the use case space for QAOA to more general quantum Hamiltonians, where the goal is to approximate the ground-state. Snapshot-QAOA retains the desirable variational-algorithm qualities of QAOA such as a small parameter count and relatively shallow circuit depth. Snapshot-QAOA is thus a better trainable alternative to the (Near Intermediate-Scale Quantum) NISQ-era Variational Quantum Eigensolver (VQE) algorithm, while retaining a significant circuit-depth advanta…

item.title: Superconducting altermagnets could carry spin without energy loss
item.description:
📚 Physics News
Researchers have proposed that a newly identified class of magnetic materials could extend the zero-resistance currents of superconductors to electron spins. Publishing their calculations in Physical Review X, Kyle Monkman and colleagues at the University of British Columbia propose how "altermagnets" could enable persistent spin currents to flow without dissipation. If confirmed experimentally, the effect could provide a powerful new platform for spintronics, where information is encoded in spin rather than electric charge.

item.title: [ASAP] Light-Induced Ordered Pattern Formation in 2D Copper Halide Perovskites
item.description:
📚 Nano Letters: Latest Articles (ACS Publications)
👤 Atreyie Ghosh, Joseph L. Spellberg, Matthew Du, Yixuan Dou, Arashdeep S. Thind, Shengdong Qi, Janek Rieger, Calvin Raab, Suriyanarayanan Vaikuntanathan, Robert F. Klie, Lina Quan, and Sarah B. King
Nano Letters DOI: 10.1021/acs.nanolett.6c00133
